purpose: Primary public-source inventory for building the national festival master and joining performance, finance and procurement layers.

sources.observed_detail: The page states festival schedules may change by local government decision and lists annual regional festival information for 2026 through 2017.

sources.coverage: current standard dataset; quarterly update; 229 provider datasets observed
sources.observed_detail: The portal says the dataset covers representative regional culture, art, heritage, tradition, ecology, nature and local-character festivals and was modified on 2026-02-10.
sources.use: Current geocoding and duplicate-disambiguation overlay, not historical panel.

sources.coverage: field-rich XLSX snapshot; 1,214 rows on portal page
sources.access_mode: public file metadata and MCST download URL
sources.observed_detail: The portal describes the XLSX as useful for policy, research, tourism, regional economy and cultural-content analysis; it lists registration 2025-04-29 and modification 2025-08-20.
sources.use: Field-rich master supplement and validation source.

sources.coverage: culture-tourism festival performance overlay, not all festivals
sources.access_mode: public dashboard and downloads where exposed
sources.observed_detail: Use as trend evidence; do not treat mobile/card/SNS indicators as exact gate counts.
sources.use: Performance and demand-surge signals for selected festivals.

sources.coverage: fiscal year 2012 onward for disclosed event/festival cost accounting
sources.access_mode: public metadata and linked API/page; API traffic policy applies
sources.observed_detail: The portal states disclosure is based on Local Finance Act Article 60 and applies to large events, with metro 500m KRW and basic local government 300m KRW thresholds.
sources.use: Budget efficiency and net-cost analysis for large disclosed festivals.

sources.access_mode: public metadata and linked API/page; API traffic policy applies
sources.observed_detail: The portal describes four budget statistics categories: 201-03, 301-11, 307-04 and 401-04.
sources.use: Local-government budget context and potential budget-pressure signal.

sources.access_mode: public metadata and linked JSON/XML page; API traffic policy applies
sources.observed_detail: The portal lists JSON+XML link-type API, real-time update cycle and public/free use.

sources.coverage: bid notice metadata and public procurement attachments where available
sources.access_mode: public metadata; automated API may require service key; attachments may be direct or guarded
sources.observed_detail: Use G2B notice number and attachment URL as relationship evidence anchors.
